content:
# Hebrews Chapter 8 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/HEB_008_page_001.jpg

1. Now the main point in what is being said [is]: we have such a high priest, who sat down at [the] right [hand] of the throne of the Majesty in the heavens,

2. a minister of the sanctuary and of the true tabernacle, which the Lord pitched, not man.

3. For every high priest is appointed to offer both gifts and sacrifices; therefore [it is] necessary [that] this [one] also have something to offer.

4. If indeed therefore he were on earth, he would not even be a priest, since there are those offering the gifts according to the law,

5. who serve [as] a copy and shadow of the heavenly things, just as Moses was warned [when] about to complete the tabernacle: "See," he says, "that you make all things according to the pattern shown to you on the mountain."

6. But now he has obtained a more excellent ministry, by as much as he is also [the] mediator of a better covenant, which has been enacted on better promises.

7. For if that first [covenant] had been faultless, no place would have been sought for a second.

8. For finding fault with them, he says: "Behold, days are coming, says [the] Lord, and I will complete upon the house of Israel and upon the house of Judah a new covenant,

9. not according to the covenant which I made with their fathers in [the] day [when] I took hold of their hand to lead them out of [the] land of Egypt, because they did not remain in my covenant, and I disregarded them, says [the] Lord.

10. For this [is] the covenant which I will make with the house of Israel after those days, says [the] Lord: putting my laws into their mind, and upon their hearts I will write them, and I will be to them a God, and they will be to me a people.

11. And they shall not teach each one his fellow citizen and each one his brother, saying, 'Know the Lord,' for all shall know me, from [the] least of them to [the] greatest of them.

12. For I will be merciful to their unrighteousness, and their sins I will remember no more."

13. In saying "new," he has made the first old. And what is becoming old and aging [is] near to disappearing.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.5 — "Copy and shadow":** hypodeigmati kai skia — the earthly tabernacle is a copy (hypo-deigma, "under-showing") and shadow (skia) of the heavenly reality. Platonic language repurposed for biblical theology.

2. **vv.8-12 — Jeremiah 31:31-34:** The longest OT quotation in the NT (also the longest in Hebrews). The new covenant prophecy — God writes the law on hearts rather than stone.

3. **v.13 — "Near to disappearing":** engys aphanismou — the old covenant is not yet gone but is "near to vanishing." This temporal language may date the letter before 70 CE (the temple's destruction).

— EMET, AI Translator
